flashcards.q: What is the term for the variety of plants and animals in a region?
What is the method of arranging things based on common features?
Which type of plants have short, green, and tender stems?
Plants with hard, thin stems branching near the ground are called what?
Definition: Tall plants with thick, woody, brown stems branching high up.
What are plants with weak stems that need support called?
What name is given to plants that spread along the ground?
What is the net-like pattern of veins on a leaf called?
What type of venation is seen in banana leaves and grasses?
A root system with one main root and small side roots is called what?
What type of roots look like a bunch of similar-sized thin roots?
Seeds that can be split into two parts are called what?
Maize and wheat are examples of which seed type?
Dicot plants usually have which root system?
Monocot plants usually have which leaf venation?
What body parts do fish use for movement in water?
What do we call the place where plants and animals naturally live?
Name the two main types of habitats.
Animals that live on both land and water are known as what?
What are special features that enable survival in a particular region called?
How do cacti store water in the desert?
flashcards.a: In their thick and fleshy stems.

flashcards.q: Why do mountain trees often have a conical shape?
flashcards.a: To let snow slide off easily.

flashcards.q: How many humps does a cold-desert (Ladakh) camel have?
Who is known as the 'Birdman of India'?
Who was the botanist involved in the 'Save Silent Valley' movement?
When was 'Project Tiger' initiated in India?
What are traditionally protected patches of forest called?
Which Indian state is home to the hot desert camels with wide hooves?
True or False: Desert animals like camels sweat a lot to keep cool.
flashcards.a: False; they do not sweat to conserve water.

flashcards.q: Why do fish have streamlined bodies?
flashcards.a: To help them swim easily in water.
